chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 4: Intent Artifacts: The Core Mechanism
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the fourth chapter of the IVD book. Teaches what an
# intent artifact is (YAML structure), the four levels, essential sections,
# placement, and a hands-on walkthrough for the first module-level intent.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_1_foundations.chapters[3]
# Source:    templates/intent.yaml, intent_levels_guide.md, framework.md
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-01-26
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Teach the intent artifact as the core mechanism of IVD: YAML structure, four levels (System, Workflow, Module, Task), essential sections (scope, intent, constraints, verification), where intent lives, and a hands-on walkthrough for writing the first module-level intent."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Knows what an intent artifact is—a structured YAML contract that AI writes, implements against, and verifies
    2. Understands the four levels (System, Workflow, Module, Task) and when to use each (decision tree; placement rules)
    3. Can identify and use essential sections: scope, intent, constraints, verification (and rationale, alternatives, risks where appropriate)
    4. Has written or followed a complete walkthrough of their first module-level intent
    5. Understands the flow from intent → implementation → verification and common mistakes to avoid
    6. Knows where to find templates and quick reference (template library, Appendix A)
    
    This chapter is the bridge from principles (Ch3) to practice (Part II). Reader leaves able to create and place intent artifacts correctly.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can describe the anatomy of an intent artifact (scope, intent, constraints, verification at minimum)
    - Reader can choose the right level using the decision tree (system vs workflow vs module vs task)
    - Reader knows placement: system at root; workflow in workflows/ or alongside coordinator when single-orchestrator; module/task co-located with implementation
    - Reader has seen or done a full module-level intent walkthrough
    - Reader can name 2–3 common mistakes and how to fix them
    - Chapter ends with clear bridge to Part II (practice projects)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1 purpose: establish the paradigm)"
    - "templates (canonical structure and levels)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch4)
# ===================================================================

dule/task co-located with implementation
    - Reader has seen or done a full module-level intent walkthrough
    - Reader can name 2–3 common mistakes and how to fix them
    - Chapter ends with clear bridge to Part II (practice projects)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1 purpose: establish the paradigm)"
    - "templates (canonical structure and levels)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch4)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "4.1"
      title: "Anatomy of an Intent Artifact"
      required_content:
        - "What an intent artifact is: structured YAML contract between human and AI (and AI with itself)"
        - "Core idea: AI writes it, AI implements against it, AI verifies against constraints (Principle 6)"
        - "Essential sections at all levels: scope, intent, constraints, verification (minimum for any intent)"
        - "Scope: level (system|workflow|module|task), type, granularity"
        - "Intent: summary, goal, success_metric, stakeholders"
        - "Constraints: requirement + test path (executable); consequence_if_violated (Principle 2)"
        - "Verification: how to verify (test_cases, property_tests for task-level; CI for continuous)"
        - "Full Layered Understanding (Principle 5): also rationale, alternatives, risks—required per ivd_system_intent (required_fields for each layer); minimum viable intent can start with scope, intent, constraints, verification, then add layers"
        - "Level-specific sections: workflow (steps, data_flow, error_handling) for workflow-level; interface, behavior for task-level"
        - "Optional: metadata (feature_id, category, tags, status) for module-level in large projects—derived feature inventory, no separate inventory file (ivd_system_intent)"
        - "Optional for data-heavy intents: field_mapping (sources, targets, mappings, transformations, verification) for workflow/module intents involving data integration, ETL, or API sync—makes field mappings verifiable (recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml)"
        - "Optional for system-level in existing projects: project_context (code_rules, architecture, tools, libraries, key_paths 'map to the stars')—created by ivd_init tool; covered in detail in Chapter 17"
        - "Optional when Principle 8 applies: inversion_opportunities (problem, dominant_belief, proposed_inversions with status chosen|rejected|deferred)"
        - "Reference: templates/intent.yaml as canonical structure; no invented sections that contradict IVD"
    
    - id: "4.2"
      title: "Choosing the Right Level and Where Intent Lives"
      required_content:
        - "Decision tree: What am I documenting? → Entire product → System; Multi-step process (3+ steps) → Workflow; Feature/component → Module; Single function/API → Task"
        - "Four levels summarized with scope and key sections (table or list)"
        - "Placement rules (canon from framework.md, intent_levels_guide.md):"
        - "System: at repo roo

proposed_inversions with status chosen|rejected|deferred)"
        - "Reference: templates/intent.yaml as canonical structure; no invented sections that contradict IVD"
    
    - id: "4.2"
      title: "Choosing the Right Level and Where Intent Lives"
      required_content:
        - "Decision tree: What am I documenting? → Entire product → System; Multi-step process (3+ steps) → Workflow; Feature/component → Module; Single function/API → Task"
        - "Four levels summarized with scope and key sections (table or list)"
        - "Placement rules (canon from framework.md, intent_levels_guide.md):"
        - "System: at repo root — system_intent.yaml or {project}_system_intent.yaml"
        - "Workflow: recommended workflows/{name}_intent.yaml for discoverability; alternative: alongside coordinator when single-orchestrator and not referenced elsewhere"
        - "Module: co-located with implementation — {module}/{module}_intent.yaml (REQUIRED pattern for tool discovery; not bare intent.yaml)"
        - "Task: co-located in intents/ subfolder — {module}/intents/{task}_intent.yaml (REQUIRED for level detection; not flat)"
        - "Linking hierarchy: parent_intent field to link child→parent (task→module, module→workflow/system, workflow→system)"
        - "When to use workflow-level: 3+ steps across files, AI needs process understanding, onboarding needs flow visibility"
        - "When NOT to over-document: task-level for 10–20% of critical functions; don't create intent for every function"
        - "Frequency guide: system 1 per product; workflow 1 per multi-step process; module 1 per feature; task only for critical functions"
        - "Large projects: optional metadata (feature_id, category, tags, status) on module intents for derived feature inventory (framework.md, ivd_system_intent)"
    
    - id: "4.3"
      title: "Writing Your First Module-Level Intent"
      required_content:
        - "Hands-on walkthrough: pick a small module or feature the reader can relate to (e.g. small API, one agent, one component)"
        - "Step-by-step: (1) scope (level=module, type, granularity), (2) parent_intent (link to system/workflow), (3) intent (summary, goal, success_metric), (4) constraints with test paths, (5) verification"
        - "Include at least one complete or near-complete YAML example (module-level) inline or in a code block"
        - "Show parent_intent usage: how to link module to system intent or workflow intent (relative path, why it matters)"
        - "Emphasize: AI writes the intent from human description; human reviews 'Is this what I meant?' then AI implements and verifies"
        - "Optional: link to templates/intent.yaml or templates/examples; reference recipe if following a pattern (e.g. agent-classifier)"
        - "What if I don't understand the concepts? Use teaching first (framework Step 0a, ivd_teach_concept); AI explains, you confirm understanding (Principle 6 teaching)"
        - "What if I don't know what to build? Use discovery first (framework Step 0b, ivd_discover_goal, list recipes); choose a direction, then write first module-level intent (Principle 6 discovery)"
    
    - id: "4.4"
      title: "From

description; human reviews 'Is this what I meant?' then AI implements and verifies"
        - "Optional: link to templates/intent.yaml or templates/examples; reference recipe if following a pattern (e.g. agent-classifier)"
        - "What if I don't understand the concepts? Use teaching first (framework Step 0a, ivd_teach_concept); AI explains, you confirm understanding (Principle 6 teaching)"
        - "What if I don't know what to build? Use discovery first (framework Step 0b, ivd_discover_goal, list recipes); choose a direction, then write first module-level intent (Principle 6 discovery)"
    
    - id: "4.4"
      title: "From Intent to Implementation (the flow)"
      required_content:
        - "The IVD flow: Describe (human) → AI writes intent → Review (human) → AI implements → AI verifies (constraint tests)"
        - "Bidirectional sync: changes to intent, code, or docs trigger verification (Principle 3); continuous verification (Principle 4)"
        - "Where implementation lives: implementation section in intent (current path, tests path, version)"
        - "Reader understands that intent is the source of truth; code and docs are derived and must align"
    
    - id: "4.5"
      title: "Common Mistakes and How to Fix Them"
      required_content:
        - "Constraint without test path → add test: path/to/test.py::test_name; constraint must be executable (Principle 2)"
        - "Wrong level: e.g. task-level detail in module intent → split into module + task-level intents; or module-level in system → move down"
        - "Intent written after code / intent describes HOW → intent first; intent describes WHAT/WHY; refactor prose to behavior and constraints"
        - "Placement wrong: e.g. all intents in one /intents folder → co-locate with implementation; use workflows/ for workflow-level"
        - "Missing verification: no test path or no CI → add test path to every constraint; run constraint tests in CI"
        - "Over-documentation: task-level for every function → only 10–20% critical functions; use highest level that captures necessary detail"
        - "Skipping inversion when it applies: designing new/major intent with conventional default without considering Principle 8 (invert, evaluate)—when stakes matter (perf, scale, security), document inversion_opportunities or rationale for chosen approach"
        - "At least 3–5 concrete mistakes with fix; reader can self-check"
    
    - id: "4.6"
      title: "Template Library and Quick Reference"
      required_content:
        - "Where to find templates: templates/intent.yaml (canonical), templates/examples (workflow, task-level examples)"
        - "Intent levels guide: templates/intent_levels_guide.md (canonical path)—decision tree, placement, frequency"
        - "Quick reference: Appendix A (IVD Quick Reference) — intent artifact template, levels decision tree, verification checklist"
        - "Recipes: pointer to recipe library (Appendix B, recipes/) for patterns (agent-classifier, workflow-orchestration, etc.) when applicable"
        - "Bridge to Part II: 'In Part II you will build real projects using these artifacts; start with Project 1 (Chapter 5).'"
  
  manuscr

mplates: templates/intent.yaml (canonical), templates/examples (workflow, task-level examples)"
        - "Intent levels guide: templates/intent_levels_guide.md (canonical path)—decision tree, placement, frequency"
        - "Quick reference: Appendix A (IVD Quick Reference) — intent artifact template, levels decision tree, verification checklist"
        - "Recipes: pointer to recipe library (Appendix B, recipes/) for patterns (agent-classifier, workflow-orchestration, etc.) when applicable"
        - "Bridge to Part II: 'In Part II you will build real projects using these artifacts; start with Project 1 (Chapter 5).'"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch4 key_points: what an intent artifact is, four levels, essential sections, first intent walkthrough"
      - "At least one complete or near-complete YAML example (module-level) in chapter"
      - "Decision tree or clear table for level selection and placement"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Checklist where it adds value (e.g. pre-flight before writing intent, or verification checklist)—book_system_intent.chapter_takeaways"
      - "Clear bridge to Part II / Chapter 5"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch4; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "An intent artifact is a structured YAML contract: scope, intent, constraints, verification (essential); rationale, alternatives, risks (layered)"
  - "Four levels: System (product root), Workflow (multi-step process), Module (feature/component), Task (critical function/API)"
  - "Placement: system at root; workflow in workflows/ or alongside coordinator when single-orchestrator; module {module}_intent.yaml (not bare intent.yaml); task in intents/ subfolder (required for tooling)"
  - "Linking: parent_intent establishes hierarchy (task→module, module→workflow/system, workflow→system); enables context inheritance and navigation"
  - "Your first intent: module-level walkthrough—scope → parent_intent → intent → constraints → verification; AI writes, human reviews, AI implements and verifies"
  - "Common mistakes: constraint without test path, wrong level, intent after code, wrong placement (bare intent.yaml, flat task), missing parent_intent, missing verification, over-documentation—and how to fix them"
  - "Templates and quick reference: templates/intent.yaml, intent_levels_guide, Appendix A; bridge to Part II practice"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"

verification, over-documentation—and how to fix them"
  - "Templates and quick reference: templates/intent.yaml, intent_levels_guide, Appendix A; bridge to Part II practice"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
  
  - name: "canonical_artifact_structure"
    requirement: "Intent artifact structure, levels, and placement must align with templates/intent.yaml, templates/intent_levels_guide.md, framework.md (placement). Layered Understanding (Principle 5): rationale, alternatives, risks are required for full compliance per ivd_system_intent; minimum viable = scope, intent, constraints, verification."
    source: "IVD framework templates, intent_levels_guide.md, ivd_system_intent.yaml"
    validation: "No invented sections or placement rules that contradict IVD; level names and placement match canon; Principle 5 layers described per framework"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml (canon consistency)"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch4 aligns with ivd_system_intent.yaml and templates; no contradictions"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 4 is the first concrete 'how' for the artifact itself—structure, levels, placement, first intent. Part I sequence: Ch1 problem, Ch2 paradigm, Ch3 principles, Ch4 mechanism (artifact)."
  
  reasoning: |
    -

ays, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 4 is the first concrete 'how' for the artifact itself—structure, levels, placement, first intent. Part I sequence: Ch1 problem, Ch2 paradigm, Ch3 principles, Ch4 mechanism (artifact)."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part I purpose: establish the paradigm; Ch4 closes the loop by teaching the core mechanism (the artifact) so the reader can apply principles in Part II.
    - Anatomy first (4.1) so the reader knows what they're building; level choice and placement (4.2) so they put intents in the right place; walkthrough (4.3) for immediate applicability.
    - Flow (4.4) ties back to Principles 3–4 (bidirectional sync, continuous verification); common mistakes (4.5) prevent the most frequent failures; template library (4.6) supports dip-in and Appendix A.
    - Placement rules come from framework.md and intent_levels_guide.md (canon)—recommended workflows/, alternative alongside coordinator when single-orchestrator; module/task co-located.
    - One hands-on module-level walkthrough is sufficient for "first intent"; Part II projects (Ch5–10) provide deeper practice at each level.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1, Ch4 key_points, placeholder); templates/intent.yaml, intent_levels_guide.md, framework.md, ivd_system_intent.yaml (principles 2,5,8; metadata)"
  date: "2026-01-26"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-04-intent-artifacts/chapter_04_intent_artifacts_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-04-intent-artifacts/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch4 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (4.1–4.6) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary; optional Checklist (p

ate_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch4 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (4.1–4.6) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary; optional Checklist (pre-flight or verification)"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, deliverables.manuscript.required_elements"
    - check: "At least one module-level YAML example; decision tree or table for level/placement"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[4.1], [4.2], [4.3]"
    - check: "Placement rules match framework.md and intent_levels_guide.md (system root, workflows/ or coordinator, co-locate module/task)"
      validates: "constraints.canonical_artifact_structure"
    - check: "Intent artifact structure (scope, intent, constraints, verification) matches templates/intent.yaml; no contradictory sections"
      validates: "constraints.canonical_artifact_structure, constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    - check: "Principle 5 (Layered Understanding): rationale, alternatives, risks described as required for full compliance; minimum viable intent clearly distinguished"
      validates: "constraints.canonical_artifact_structure, ivd_system_intent principle_5"
    - check: "Optional metadata (large projects) and inversion_opportunities (Principle 8) mentioned where relevant (4.1 and/or 4.2, 4.5)"
      validates: "ivd_system_intent (metadata, principle_8)"
    - check: "Section 4.6 points to templates (intent.yaml, intent_levels_guide.md) and Appendix A; bridge to Part II / Ch5"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[4.6]"
